title: podman  ps --format status=stopped is invalid
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

These appear to be the supported libpod/podman states:
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/993b3a17c58826365136f49d7760f8ac88e320b2/pkg/adapter/pods_remote.go#L246-L257

However, the stopped state is marked as invalid when trying to query it. Even the manual page suggests it should be a supported option.

```
$ man podman-ps | grep Status
       │.Status     │ Status of container                      │
       │status   │ [Status] Container's status, e.g running, stopped                   │
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `$ podman ps -f "status=stopped"`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman ps -f "status=stopped"
invalid filter: stopped is not a valid status
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ podman ps -f "status=stopped"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This was originally brought up in this PR: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2524

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Mon Jan 14 15:38:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  git commit: '"49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"'

  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora

  MemFree: 22500847616

  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-68.dev.git6635b4f.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ef9132178ccc3d2775d4fb51f1e431f30cac1398-dirty

  SwapFree: 5368705024
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical (laptop).

comments.body:
On a somewhat related note, querying for the `restarting` state appears to work but might be a false-positive since libpod does not fully support this state. Maybe this should be marked invalid?

```
$ podman ps -f status=restarting
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```
Going to try and take a look at this today, time permitting

(I've actually just mapped the Stopped state to Exited in that PR - they're sufficiently similar as to not really merit filtering separately. Stopped is an intermediate that that quickly transitions to Exited.

Reopening, because although #2586 does handle some corner cases, it doesn't actually address the issue raised in this PR: `--filter=stopped` is still rejected. (And, as @ekultails noted, `stopped` is the second example in the man page). (And, it's a valid state per `libpod/container.go`)

@edsantiago I could make `stopped` map to `exited` as well, which might solve this.

(They are distinct states in libpod, but we transition from `stopped` to `exited` as soon as the cleanup process runs - less than a second, probably)

@mheon I interpret your suggestion as: "I could silently translate the string `stopped`, when given as a command-line input to the `state` filter, into `exited`". If that's your intention, it sounds like a good solution to me.
